cause: Invalid pointer, null dereference, or buffer overflow
solution: Validate all pointers before use. Check array bounds. Use debugger or serial output to identify crash location.

cause: Too large local variables, deep recursion, or insufficient stack size
solution: Reduce local buffer sizes. Convert to dynamic allocation or global variables. Check for infinite recursion. Increase stack size if using RTOS.
Use static allocation. Pool similar-sized objects. Pre-allocate buffers. Consider custom allocator.

cause: Missing exit condition or increment in wrong place
solution: Add debug prints. Verify loop conditions. Ensure loop variable is incremented/modified correctly.

cause: Concurrent access to shared resources without synchronization
solution: Use mutexes/semaphores. Disable interrupts during critical sections. Use atomic operations for simple variables.

cause: Circular wait on locks or resources
solution: Always acquire locks in same order. Use timeouts on lock attempts. Avoid nested locks when possible.

cause: Misaligned memory access or bus timeout
solution: Ensure data alignment for DMA operations. Check peripheral connections. Verify bus timing.

cause: Invalid memory access or undefined instruction on ARM
solution: Enable fault handlers. Check stack pointer. Validate pointer arithmetic. Use MPU if available.

cause: Math operation exceeds data type range
solution: Use larger data types (long, unsigned long). Check for overflow before operations. Use saturation arithmetic if appropriate.

cause: Division by zero or invalid floating point operation
solution: Check divisors before division. Validate input ranges. Consider fixed-point math for critical operations.
Always check array bounds. Use safe string functions (strncpy, snprintf). Consider using std::vector or safe string libraries.

cause: Variable used before being assigned a value
solution: Initialize variables on declaration. Enable compiler warnings (-Wall). Set default values in setup().
Clear interrupt flags in handler. Add debouncing for GPIO. Verify interrupt enable settings.

cause: Timing issues, power supply noise, or signal reflection
solution: Add decoupling capacitors (0.1uF) near power pins. Use proper grounding. Add pull-up/pull-down resistors.

cause: I2C/SPI bus hang or sensor unresponsive
solution: Implement bus timeout. Add watchdog. Reset sensor in code. Check power supply to sensor.
